title: Fix CI: Node20 deprecation + golangci-lint Go 1.26 incompat
description: GitHub Actions warns Node20 deprecation (checkout@v4, setup-go@v5 forced to Node24). golangci-lint 1.64.8 built with go1.24 cannot target go1.26.3. Bump actions to Node24 majors and migrate to golangci-lint v2 (action v8 + v2 config schema).
close_reason: Bumped checkout@v5/setup-go@v6/golangci-lint-action@v9 (Node24), migrated golangci-lint to v2, fixed all 71 lint findings. CI green, no warnings.

title: Fix module path: rampamac -> rampa069
description: go.mod module path and all import paths use github.com/rampamac/ft8copilot but the correct GitHub username is rampa069. Rename across go.mod, all .go files, and README install instruction.
close_reason: Renamed module path rampamac->rampa069 across go.mod, all .go files, README. Build/vet/tests pass.

title: my_continent global / autodeducido del indicativo (no por-selector)
description:
El filtro 'ignoring DX calling own continent' en internal/selector/base.go usa my_continent con defaultContinent='NA' hardcodeado, y my_continent solo es configurable POR SELECTOR (config.SelectorConfig.MyContinent). Para un operador europeo (p.ej. EA5IUE) el defecto NA es incorrecto: toda estacion de Norteamerica que llama 'CQ DX' (Extra=DX, Continent=NA) coincide con NA y se descarta en TODOS los selectores. El usuario lo ha notado: ignora USA/Canada/Mexico que llaman CQ DX.

Objetivo: que my_continent sea un ajuste global (seccion ft8ctrl) y, si no se especifica, se autodeduzca del indicativo my_call via dxcc.Lookup. El override por-selector (SelectorConfig.MyContinent) se mantiene con maxima prioridad.
design:
- Anadir MyContinent string yaml:my_continent a config.FT8Ctrl.
- Anadir Continent string a selector.Deps.
- En main.go (arranque y recarga SIGHUP): operatorContinent = cfg.FT8Ctrl.MyContinent; si vacio, entities.Lookup(my_call).Continent; fallback NA. Pasar a Deps.Continent.
- En NewBase: continent = defaultContinent; si deps.Continent!='' usarlo; si cfg.MyContinent!='' (por-selector) tiene prioridad final.
- Quitar las lineas my_continent: EU por-selector del ft8ctrl.yaml (ya no necesarias).
acceptance_criteria:
- Con my_call=EA5IUE y sin my_continent configurado, una estacion NA llamando CQ DX se considera candidata (no se descarta).
- ft8ctrl.my_continent override funciona globalmente.
- my_continent por-selector sigue teniendo prioridad.
- SIGHUP recalcula el continente. Tests anaden cobertura.
notes: Reportado por EA5IUE: ignoraba NA (USA/Canada/Mexico) y se sospecha que afecta a otras zonas.
close_reason: Implementado: my_continent global en seccion ft8ctrl + autodeduccion desde my_call via dxcc.Lookup (EA5IUE -> EU). Override por-selector mantiene prioridad. Aplicado en arranque y en recarga SIGHUP. Quitadas las lineas my_continent por-selector del yaml. Tests anadidos, README y sample documentados.

title: Sequencer daemon (UDP loop, message parser, TX sequencing, retries)
description: Port the Sequencer from ft8ctrl.py: bind the WSJT-X UDP socket, decode incoming packets, parse decoded messages (CQ / BROKENCQ / REPLY regexes), and drive automatic TX. On CQ -> enqueue DB INSERT; on REPLY to someone else for the call we're working -> HaltTx and DELETE; on QSOLogged -> mark worked + forward to optional secondary logger; on Status -> track frequency/TX state, enforce tx_retries, and on the FT8/FT4 sequence-second boundaries ask the selector for the best station and send a Reply packet (call_station) with optional SHIFT modifier for follow-frequency. Replace select()/threads with Go net + goroutines/channels.
notes: internal/sequencer: port of Sequencer (ft8ctrl.py). sequencer.go: New(cfg,chain,cmds,log) binds WSJT-X UDP socket (+optional secondary logger DialUDP); Run(ctx) loop reads with 0.7s deadline (emulates select timeout) then sequenceCheck. handlePacket type-switch: Heartbeat/ADIF ignored; QSOLogged->logCall+current=''; DecodeMsg->handleDecode; Status->handleStatus. handleDecode: parseMessage; REPLY to other (call==current && to!=mycall)->stopTransmit+DeleteCmd; CQ->InsertCmd(Spot+Packet). handleStatus: txTracker.observe(retries/last_tx_message) ->exceeded halts; sets sequence (FT8/FT4 seconds), frequency, txStatus=Transmitting||TXEnabled; Transmitting&&DXCall->StatusCmd status=1. sequenceCheck: when !txStatus and now.Second in sequence (lastSecond guard replaces time.sleep(1)), chain.Select(band)->callStation(Reply, SHIFT if follow_frequency)+current+tracker.reset. logCall: forwardToLogger (TXPower stamp + '[ft8ctrl] ' comment) + StatusCmd status=2. parser.go: REPLY/CQ/BROKENCQ regexes; RE2 has no lookahead so REPLY '(?!CQ)' emulated by rejecting to=='CQ'. sequence.go: SEQUENCE_TIME sets + txTracker. send() non-blocking (drops if writer backed up) to protect UDP loop. 9 tests pass (parser, txTracker, sequence set, handleDecode CQ/reply-to-other/reply-to-us, handleStatus, sequenceCheck select/skip).
close_reason: Sequencer daemon (UDP loop, parser, TX sequencing, retries, logger forward) implemented and tested (9 tests); package builds/vets clean.

title: Project scaffolding & build tooling
description: Set up the Go module and repository layout for the port. Create go.mod (module path), a cmd/ layout for the three binaries (ft8ctrl, lookup, countries) and internal/ packages (wsjtx, config, geo, dxcc, db, lotw, selector). Add Makefile/build scripts, golangci-lint config, .gitignore, and GitHub Actions CI mirroring the existing pre-commit/flake8/pylint setup. Pick SQLite (modernc.org/sqlite pure-Go vs mattn/go-sqlite3) and YAML (gopkg.in/yaml.v3) libraries and record the decision.
notes: Module: github.com/rampamac/ft8copilot (Go 1.26). Layout: cmd/{ft8ctrl,lookup,countries} + internal/{wsjtx,config,geo,dxcc,db,lotw,selector,log}, each seeded with a doc.go pointing to its port task. Toolchain: modernc.org/sqlite (pure-Go, no cgo, static cross-compile) + gopkg.in/yaml.v3. Added Makefile, .golangci.yml, GitHub Actions CI (fmt/vet/test/build + lint), README, LICENSE (BSD-3, W6BSD attribution), ft8ctrl.yaml.sample carried over, .gitignore. Verified: gofmt clean, go vet clean, make build produces all 3 binaries, smoke run OK.
close_reason: Scaffolding complete: module, layout, deps, build tooling, CI, docs all in place and verified building.

title: WSJT-X UDP protocol codec (Qt datastream + packet types)
description: Port wsjtx.py: the QDataStream-compatible binary encoder/decoder for the WSJT-X UDP protocol (magic 0xADBCCBDA, schema 2). Implement big-endian primitives (bool/byte/uint16/uint32/int32/quint64/double, QString with -1 null length, QDateTime with Julian day + msecs + timespec). Implement the packet types used by the app: Heartbeat(0), Status(1), Decode(2), Clear(3), Reply(4), QSOLogged(5), Close(6), HaltTx(8), FreeText(9), LoggedADIF(12), HighlightCallsign(13), plus the ft8_decode dispatcher and Mode/SOMode/Modifiers enums. Include Julian<->time and Maidenhead-grid time helpers. This is the foundational network layer.
notes: internal/wsjtx implemented. Files: consts.go (Magic/Schema/client ids, PacketType, Mode '~'/'+', Modifier, SOMode enums), codec.go (big-endian reader/writer with sticky-error reader, QString null=-1, QDateTime, Julian<->time.Time, wstime<->time.Time helpers), messages.go (Decode dispatcher + types). Decode handles Heartbeat/Status/Decode/Clear/QSOLogged/Close/ADIF; Encode on Heartbeat/Reply/QSOLogged/Close/HaltTx/FreeText/HighlightCallsign. Decode returns ErrNotWSJTX (bad magic) or *UnknownTypeError. Parity notes: Reply uses client id 'AUTOFT' (vs default 'AUTOFS') as in original; null QString decodes to '' (lossy vs Python None, irrelevant to app paths); offset-from-UTC read but ignored. 8 codec tests pass (round-trips + julian/wstime + error paths). Comprehensive WSJT-X capture parity tests deferred to .15.
close_reason: WSJT-X UDP codec + packet types implemented and unit-tested (8 tests pass, vet/build clean).

title: Port FT8Commander (WSJT-X FT8 automation) to Go
description: Port the Python project ../FT8Commander (github.com/0x9900/FT8Commander) to Go. It is an experimental WSJT-X FT8/FT4 automation daemon that listens to WSJT-X UDP packets, records CQ-calling stations into a SQLite DB (with DXCC, geo distance/azimuth, LOTW enrichment), and automatically replies to the station with the best chance of completing a QSO using configurable selector plugins. The Go port must remain protocol- and config-compatible with WSJT-X 2.5+ and the existing ft8ctrl.yaml. Deliverables: a ft8ctrl daemon plus lookup and countries CLI helpers. Runs on macOS and Linux.

title: TUI: borde superior del marco desplazado un carácter a la derecha en ventanas modales (search, ayuda, parámetros)
description: En el TUI, la línea superior del marco (borde) aparece desplazada un carácter a la derecha respecto al resto del recuadro. Se observa en la ventana de búsqueda (search) y también en las ventanas de ayuda y de parámetros. El borde superior no queda alineado con los bordes laterales/inferior del marco.

title: cmd/adif: import/export subcommands + export logic + docs
description: Restructure cmd/adif into subcommands: 'adif [-c cfg] import <file> [--dry-run]' (move existing import logic) and 'adif [-c cfg] export [--all] [--band N] [--status N] <file|->'. Export selects rows via Store (default status=2 worked; --all = any; --band/--status filters), maps each db.Record to an adif.Record (call, qso_date+time_on from Time, band Nm, freq Hz->MHz, gridsquare, country, cqz, ituz, cont, mode defaulting to FT8), adds station_callsign/my_gridsquare from config, writes header+records via the serializer to the file (or stdout for '-'). Prints exported count + per-band. README + Makefile already lists adif. Tests: export to buffer yields valid ADIF that re-parses, status/band filtering, round-trip import->export.
notes: Done. cmd/adif restructured into subcommands with a leading global -c: 'adif import <file> [--dry-run]' (moved existing import) and 'adif export <file|-> [--all] [--band N] [--status N]'. Export selects rows via Store.Search (default status=2 worked; --all=any; --band/--status filters), maps each db.Record to an adif.Record (CALL/BAND/MODE=FT8/QSO_DATE/TIME_ON/FREQ MHz/GRIDSQUARE/COUNTRY/CONT/CQZ/ITUZ + STATION_CALLSIGN/MY_GRIDSQUARE from config), writes header+records via the encoder to a file or stdout ('-'); summary to stderr. README updated to subcommands + export section. Verified end-to-end: imported 7907 from the real log into a temp DB and exported valid ADIF. Tests: recordToADIF mapping + empties omitted, writeADIF round-trip (worked-only filter).
close_reason: import/export subcommands + export logic + docs implemented and tested

title: internal/adif: ADIF serializer/encoder
description: Add an ADIF writer to internal/adif: encode a Record (and a header) back to ADIF text. <NAME:bytelen>value fields (uppercased names, byte length = len(value) for UTF-8 correctness), empty values skipped, <eor> per record, an optional header with free-text comment + ADIF_VER/PROGRAMID tags + <eoh>. Round-trips with the parser. Tests: single field length, record with <eor>, header, empty-value skipping, parse(encode(x))==x round-trip, UTF-8 byte length.
notes: Done. internal/adif/encode.go: Encoder over an io.Writer — WriteHeader(comment, fields)+<EOH>, WriteRecord(rec)+<EOR>, Flush. Fields written as <NAME:bytelen>value (uppercased, byte length for UTF-8 correctness), empty values skipped, sorted for determinism. Tests: record+<EOR>, header, UTF-8 byte length (España->7), Parse(encode(x)) round-trip.
close_reason: ADIF serializer implemented and tested (round-trips with parser)

title: ADIF export
description: Export the cqcalls database to an ADIF file — the symmetric counterpart of the importer. Restructure cmd/adif to subcommands: 'adif import <file>' (existing logic) and 'adif export <file>'. Export writes worked rows (status=2) by default, with --all / --band / --status filters, reusing Store queries. Each row becomes an ADIF record (call, qso_date, time_on, band, freq, gridsquare, country, cqz, ituz, cont, mode); a header carries ADIF_VER/PROGRAMID and station_callsign/my_gridsquare from config. Reuses internal/adif (new serializer).
close_reason: ADIF export complete: encoder + export subcommand; verified round-trip on the real log

title: db: worked-QSO import path (DXCC enrich, grid-tolerant, status=2)
description:
Add an import path that writes a worked QSO into cqcalls reusing the Writer's DXCC enrichment, but tolerating a missing/blank grid (unlike Writer.insert which skips rows without a valid grid). For worked rows geo is optional: compute lat/lon/distance/azimuth when GRIDSQUARE parses, else store zeros. Resolve DXCC entity from the call (country/continent/cqz/ituz); on lookup failure, count+skip. Upsert keyed by (call, band) with status=2; idempotent (re-import does not duplicate or downgrade). 

Also add band mapping from an ADIF BAND name ('160m'..'70cm' -> metres) with FREQ (MHz->Hz->db.Band) as fallback. Likely a new Writer method (e.g. ImportWorked(call, band, grid, freqHz, when)) or an ImportCmd. Tests: import sets status=2, missing-grid import still stored, duplicate import idempotent, DXCC100/WorkedCountries sees imported countries, band-name parsing.
design: From the real QRZ export: records carry band (e.g. '2m'), freq (MHz, e.g. 145.7), gridsquare (may be absent), qso_date, time_on, mode, and ALSO ADIF country/dxcc/cqz/cont fields. Design: resolve DXCC from the call (consistent with the live selectors); when the call lookup FAILS, fall back to the ADIF country/cqz/cont fields if present instead of skipping — improves import coverage for odd/portable calls. Prefer exact field 'band'/'freq' (not band_rx/freq_rx).
notes: Done. db.Writer.ImportWorked(WorkedQSO) upserts a worked (status=2) row via importSQL (ON CONFLICT(call,band) DO UPDATE SET status=2 — idempotent, upgrades existing spots). Grid-tolerant: geo only computed when grid present and parses (empty grid -> (0,0) guarded). DXCC resolved from call; falls back to WorkedQSO Country/Continent/CQZone/ITUZone when lookup fails; skips (ok=false) when no country or band==0. Added db.BandMetersFromName for ADIF band names ('20m'->20, '70cm'->0). Tests: status=2, missing grid, idempotent x3, upgrades existing spot, seen by WorkedCountries (DXCC100), fallback country, skip unknown band, band-name parsing.
close_reason: Worked-QSO import path implemented and tested

title: internal/adif: streaming ADIF parser
description:
New package internal/adif with a robust, streaming ADIF parser. ADIF structure: optional header text terminated by <EOH>; then records, each a sequence of <FIELD:length[:type]>value tags terminated by <EOR>. Tag names are case-insensitive; values are exactly 'length' bytes (may contain < > and whitespace), so parse by length not delimiters. Whitespace/newlines between tags are ignored. A file with no header starts directly at records.

API sketch: a Reader over an io.Reader yielding records as map[string]string with upper-cased keys, or a Parse(io.Reader) ([]Record, error)/iterator. Be tolerant: skip malformed tags with a recoverable error, handle missing <EOH>, CRLF, trailing data after the last <EOR>.

Tests: minimal record, header+records, WSJT-X export sample, length-prefixed values containing odd chars, multiple records, missing header, empty file.
design: Real sample = QRZ Logbook export (7908 records). Notes: tags are LOWERCASE (<call:6>, <eor>, <eoh>) -> case-insensitive keys required; header is free indented text with some tags before <eoh>; values are length-prefixed and contain spaces (e.g. qsl_via len 47) -> must parse by length; many irrelevant fields (app_*, qrzcom_*, my_*). Streaming matters (370 KB / ~7900 records). Beware rx-variant fields: match exact 'band'/'freq', not 'band_rx'/'freq_rx'. A real export cannot ship as a test fixture (personal data, gitignored *.adi); create a small synthetic sample for tests.
notes: Done. internal/adif: tolerant streaming parser. Reader.Next()/Parse() yield Record (map upper-cased keys, Get() case-insensitive). Reads values by <name:len[:type]> length (handles < > and spaces in values), case-insensitive tags, optional header (skipped via <eoh>; headerless when first non-blank byte is '<'), CRLF/whitespace, tolerates final record without <eor>, bad length, empty value, empty file. Verified on the real QRZ export: 7908 records (matches <eor> count), 7844 with grid / 64 without, all with band. Tests cover QRZ sample, headerless, angle-brackets-in-value, no-trailing-eor, empty value, empty input, streaming.
close_reason: Streaming ADIF parser implemented and tested (incl. real 7908-record file)

title: ADIF log importer
description:
Import a historical ADIF log (e.g. a WSJT-X or logger export) into the cqcalls database, marking each QSO as worked (status=2). Today 'worked' status is only set when WSJT-X logs a live QSO, so the DB starts empty of history: the DXCC100 selector and 'already worked' filtering have nothing to go on until you make contacts. Importing seeds that history so the automation behaves correctly from the first run.

CLI surface (chosen): a new dedicated command 'cmd/adif':
    bin/adif -c ft8ctrl.yaml mylog.adi [--dry-run]
reporting imported / skipped / per-band counts; idempotent on re-run.

Semantics: each ADIF record -> upsert a cqcalls row keyed by (call, band) with status=2 (worked), enriched via the existing DXCC lookup. Band comes from the ADIF BAND field ('20m'->20) or FREQ (MHz->Hz->db.Band). Geo (lat/lon/distance) is computed only when GRIDSQUARE is present; absent grids are fine (distance/grid are unused for worked rows — DXCC100 only needs country+band+status). DXCC lookup failures are counted and skipped.
close_reason: ADIF importer complete: streaming parser, grid-tolerant worked-QSO import path, cmd/adif command + docs. Verified on a real 7908-record QRZ export.

title: TUI modal background not filled to window edges (black gaps)
description: In the F1 help screen (and likely all centred modal dialogs: search/params/candidates), the desktop background is not fully blue — areas with no text render black to the edge of the window. The background colour appears only where a string is printed; padding beyond the content stays the terminal default (black). Likely the desktop-fill style (stDesktop.Width().Height().Align().Render()) is not padding every cell with the background, or JoinVertical/line widths leave unpainted trailing cells. Fix so the whole body area is blue behind dialogs.
notes: Root cause: dialog interior cells were left with the terminal-default background (black) in three places: (1) box() padded short lines with plain spaces via fit() — the trailing pad lost the background after any embedded SGR reset (e.g. the ASCII banner, ~20 black cells/line); (2) literal ' ' separators between independently-styled segments in help/status/params rows; (3) bubbles textinput rendered its own width-padding and the hidden end-of-field cursor cell with no background. Fixes: box() now pads interiors with background-styled spaces (padInterior); separators use bgSpaces(1); the 'idle' TX label is styled; textinput Width=0 (disables its unstyled padding) and Cursor.TextStyle set to the panel background. Added TestNoUnpaintedCells regression guard (forces ANSI256 profile, asserts 0 unpainted cells in main/help/candidates/params; <=1 for the search cursor).
close_reason: Modal/panel backgrounds now fully painted; regression test added

title: TUI main model & layout: spots/candidates table, status panel, log window
description: Bubble Tea root model composing the panes: a candidate/spots table (the war-dialer scanning list), a status panel (current QSO target, band/frequency, RUNNING/PAUSED, tx retries, counts), and the scrollable log window. Wire key bindings and the function-key bar, periodic refresh tick, and resize handling. Depends on the scaffold and the log fan-out.
notes: Done. Root model composes the layout: candidates table (renderCandidates: ranked rows, chosen marked ▶ + highlighted, ineligible dimmed) | status panel (renderStatus: station/auto RUNNING-PAUSED/band/freq/TX/working/spot-counts) on the top row, log window beneath, all tiling width×height between header and F-key footer. 1s refresh tick re-queries Ranker.Rank(band) + Seq.Status(). Added sequencer.Status snapshot (atomic.Pointer published each Run iteration) + Sequencer.Status(). panes.go column/row formatting (fit/fitRight). Tests: candidates layout/dims, status RUNNING/PAUSED + counts, composed paused indicator. Snapshot verified at 84x26.
close_reason: Main model & layout composed (candidates|status + log), refresh tick, tested

title: Candidate ranking API in selector package
description: The Chain currently returns only the first winner (Chain.Select). Add a way to return the FULL ordered candidate list for a band — each candidate with its SNR, distance, coefficient, and which selector in the chain would pick it — so the TUI can show a best-to-worst 'scanning' list. Reuse Base.Candidates + coefficient + the SNR sort from SelectRecord without duplicating the blacklist/LOTW/SNR-bound filtering logic.
notes: Done. internal/selector/ranker.go: Ranker (embeds *Base, built via NewRanker(deps) with default SNR bounds + permissive LOTW). Rank(band) []Ranked returns the band's base candidate pool (same recent-spots query, own-continent-DX filter, distance/SNR coefficient as the selectors) sorted by SNR desc, each annotated Eligible/Reason (snr|blacklist|lotw) mirroring SelectRecord's predicates; the top eligible is marked Chosen. Wired into cmd/ft8ctrl (built from the same selDeps as the chain) and passed via tui.Deps.Ranker. Tests: sort+annotate+chosen, empty pool, chosen skips ineligible leader. Note: ranker uses a snapshot blacklist/lotw; rebuild on SIGHUP is a future refinement (Store is live so the pool stays fresh).
close_reason: Candidate ranking API implemented, wired, tested

title: Live parameter control API from the TUI
description: Let the TUI change hot-reloadable settings without SIGHUP: tx_power, tx_retries, follow_frequency, selector chain, BlackList, retry_time. Reuse Sequencer.Reload (rebuild selector.Chain via selector.Build) and the retryNanos atomic from cmd/ft8ctrl. Provide an in-process control surface the TUI calls. Optionally write changes back to ft8ctrl.yaml. Immutable fields (sockets/db/identity) must be rejected/greyed-out, matching warnImmutable.
notes: Done. internal/control package: Controller with Params()/Apply(Params). Apply rebuilds the selector chain (selector.Build with new chain order + blacklist), hands FT8Ctrl settings to Sequencer.Reload, updates the retryNanos atomic; on any error (e.g. unknown selector) nothing changes. Mirrors the SIGHUP reload path; only hot-reloadable fields exposed (tx_power/tx_retries/follow_frequency/retry_time/chain/blacklist). Added blacklist.Normalize (trim+upper, drop blanks). Wired in cmd/ft8ctrl (built in --tui mode from the live components) and passed via tui.Deps.Control. Tests: apply updates params+retryNanos+normalized blacklist, rejects unknown selector (no mutation), Params() returns copies. Note: YAML write-back deferred (would strip the sample's comments); changes are live in-memory.
close_reason: Live parameter control API (Controller.Apply) implemented, wired, tested

title: Sequencer pause/resume control for autopilot
description: Add a pause control to internal/sequencer (atomic bool or control channel) so sequenceCheck() becomes a no-op while paused — no station is called and no HaltTx storm — but packet ingestion (handleDecode/InsertCmd, handleStatus) keeps running so the cqcalls DB keeps filling. Expose Sequencer.Pause()/Resume()/Paused(). The single-threaded Run loop must remain the sole mutator of runtime state (follow the existing reload-channel pattern).
notes: Done. Added atomic.Bool 'paused' to Sequencer with Pause()/Resume()/TogglePause()/Paused(); sequenceCheck() returns early when paused (before consulting the chain), so no station is called while ingestion (decodes/status -> InsertCmd) keeps filling cqcalls. Toggle logs paused/resumed once per transition (Swap guard). Tests: pause gate via stubSelector (chain not consulted when paused, resumes after), toggle transitions.
close_reason: Sequencer pause/resume implemented and tested

title: Log fan-out: slog handler -> ring buffer + subscription for TUI log pane
description: internal/log currently writes to a rotating file + colorized console. Add a slog.Handler (or a tee) that also pushes formatted records into a bounded in-memory ring buffer and exposes a subscription channel so the TUI log window can stream live entries and scroll back. Keep level colorization. Must not block the daemon if the TUI is slow (drop oldest).
notes: Done. internal/log/sink.go: Sink slog.Handler with a bounded ring buffer (default 500) + non-blocking broadcast to subscribers (buffered 256, drops when behind). Snapshot() returns oldest-first copy; Subscribe() returns (<-chan Entry, cancel). Shared sinkCore so WithAttrs/WithGroup clones write to one ring; appendPlainAttr renders attrs without ANSI (TUI colours itself). log.go refactored: setup() helper, new SetupTUI(logfile)->(logger,closer,*Sink) with file@DEBUG + sink, no console. cmd/ft8ctrl wires SetupTUI in --tui mode, passes sink via tui.Deps.LogSink. internal/tui/log.go: logView (seeds from Snapshot, streams via logMsg+listen cmd, render() colours by level), wired into model Init/Update, close() on quit. Proof-of-life 'Latest log' line verified in snapshot. Tests: sink (eviction, level filter, attrs, subscribe, non-blocking, unsubscribe, with-group) + logView/model streaming.
close_reason: Log fan-out sink + subscription implemented, wired into TUI, tested end-to-end

title: Scaffold ft8ctrl --tui in-process mode + TUI framework
description: Add a '--tui' flag to cmd/ft8ctrl that boots the daemon (Store, Writer, purge, selector Chain, Sequencer goroutine) and then runs a Bubble Tea program as the main loop instead of blocking on seq.Run in the foreground. Wire graceful shutdown (ctx cancel on quit) and ensure SIGINT/SIGHUP still behave. Pull in charmbracelet/bubbletea + lipgloss + bubbles (pure Go, no cgo). This is the foundation other TUI tasks build on. Decide framework here (recommend Bubble Tea; alternative tview) and record the decision.
notes: Done. Added --tui flag to cmd/ft8ctrl: boots the daemon (store/writer/purge/chain) then runs seq.Run on a background goroutine while the Bubble Tea program owns the terminal as the main loop. Quitting the TUI cancels the daemon ctx; SIGINT tears the TUI down (tea.WithContext). Framework: charmbracelet bubbletea v1.3.10 + lipgloss v1.1.0 + bubbles v1.0.0 (pure Go, no cgo). New package internal/tui (tui.go Run/Deps, model.go scaffold model with placeholder blue frame + F-key footer). log.SetupWithConsole(logfile, console) suppresses the stderr handler in TUI mode (file still captures all). Unit tests for view/quit-keys. Verified manually by user. Visible 'BlueBEEP' text removed per request; two design-rationale code comments retain the reference.
close_reason: Scaffold complete: ft8ctrl --tui in-process mode + Bubble Tea framework, clean shutdown, tests passing, verified manually

title: TUI front-end with BlueBEEP look & feel
description:
Build an interactive terminal UI for ft8ctrl with the retro DOS aesthetic of BlueBEEP (blue background, double-line box-drawing borders + drop shadows, ANSI banner art, bottom function-key menu bar) — evoking a war-dialer / blue-box phreaking tool.

Run in-process via a new 'ft8ctrl --tui' mode: the TUI is the main loop while the existing Sequencer keeps running as a goroutine, sharing the Store, selector Chain and Sequencer. No second binary or IPC needed.

Capabilities the UI must offer:
- Live log shown inside a scrollable window/pane.
- Change daemon parameters live (tx_power, tx_retries, follow_frequency, selector chain, blacklist, retry_time) via the existing Sequencer.Reload mechanism; optionally write back to ft8ctrl.yaml.
- Pause/resume the autopilot without stopping spot ingestion (DB keeps filling).
- Search the cqcalls database (by call/country/grid/zone/status/band).
- Show the ranked candidate list (best-to-worst order) the selector chain would pick from.

Recommended framework: Bubble Tea + Lipgloss + Bubbles (Charm) — pure Go, no cgo, retro-capable. Alternative: tview.

Reference aesthetic: https://github.com/Jimvin/BlueBEEP
close_reason: All TUI children complete: scaffold, theme, log fan-out, pause, ranking, layout, search/params/candidates/help modals, docs. ft8ctrl --tui is a fully working interactive front-end.

title: Colorizar y mejorar la legibilidad de la salida de consola
description:
La salida de consola usa hoy slog.NewTextHandler a os.Stderr (internal/log/log.go:29), formato 'key=value' plano y sin color. Cuesta seguir la actividad en tiempo real (calling, logged, retries, reload...).

Objetivo:
- Colorizar la consola por nivel (DEBUG gris, INFO normal, WARN amarillo, ERROR rojo) y resaltar campos clave (call, country, band).
- Hacer la linea mas legible: timestamp corto (HH:MM:SS), nivel alineado, mensaje y luego los atributos.
- SOLO afecta a la consola; el logfile rotatorio debe seguir en texto plano (sin codigos ANSI) para no ensuciar los logs.
design:
- Implementar un slog.Handler propio para la consola (sustituye al TextHandler de stderr en Setup); el handler de fichero se mantiene como TextHandler plano dentro del fanout.
- Detectar TTY: solo emitir ANSI si os.Stderr es terminal (p.ej. golang.org/x/term IsTerminal o comprobar ModeCharDevice). Respetar NO_COLOR y permitir forzar con env (p.ej. FT8_COLOR=always/never/auto).
- Mantener el mapeo de niveles existente (parseLevel) y la opcion LOG_LEVEL.
- Considerar alinear/abreviar atributos frecuentes para QSOs (call/country/snr/distance/band/selector).
acceptance_criteria:
- En terminal interactivo la consola sale coloreada por nivel y legible.
- Redirigida a fichero/pipe (no TTY) o con NO_COLOR, la consola sale sin codigos ANSI.
- El logfile rotatorio nunca contiene secuencias ANSI.
- Tests del paquete log siguen pasando; se anade cobertura del nuevo handler (con/sin color).
notes: Solicitado por EA5IUE junto al trabajo de SIGHUP. Relacionado con internal/log/log.go.
close_reason: Implementado: handler slog propio para consola con color por nivel (DEBUG gris, INFO verde, WARN amarillo, ERROR rojo), campos clave resaltados (call/country/band), timestamp HH:MM:SS y nivel alineado. Deteccion de TTY via go-isatty, respeta NO_COLOR y FT8_COLOR=always/never. El logfile rotatorio sigue en texto plano. Tests anadidos, README actualizado.

title: Recargar configuracion en caliente con SIGHUP
description:
Permitir recargar el fichero de configuracion (ft8ctrl.yaml) sin reiniciar el daemon, enviando la senal SIGHUP al proceso.

Estado actual:
- cmd/ft8ctrl/main.go:79 solo captura SIGINT/SIGTERM via signal.NotifyContext.
- La config se carga una sola vez al arranque con config.Load (internal/config/config.go:115).

Objetivo:
- Al recibir SIGHUP, releer el fichero de config y aplicar en caliente lo que sea seguro: cadena call_selector y secciones de selectores (Any, Continent, DXCC100, etc.), BlackList, retry_time, tx_retries, tx_power, min/max_snr.
- Recrear/refrescar el pool de selectores cacheado por banda tras la recarga.
design:
Considerar:
- Que campos NO se pueden recargar en caliente sin reabrir recursos: db_name (BD abierta), wsjt_ip/wsjt_port (socket UDP), my_call/my_grid. Decidir si se ignoran, se avisa por log, o requieren reinicio.
- Validar la nueva config ANTES de aplicarla; si falla el parseo, mantener la config anterior y loguear el error (no abortar el daemon).
- Proteger el acceso concurrente a la config viva (el sequencer la lee en cada boundary). Mutex/atomic.Pointer o recarga sincronizada en el boundary.
- Invalidar el pool de selectores cacheado por banda para forzar reconstruccion.
acceptance_criteria:
- Enviar SIGHUP (kill -HUP <pid>) recarga selectores/BlackList/parametros sin matar el proceso.
- Una config invalida no tumba el daemon: se loguea y se conserva la anterior.
- Los cambios surten efecto en el siguiente ciclo FT8 sin reiniciar.
- Documentado en README (seccion de uso) que campos se recargan y cuales requieren reinicio.
notes: Solicitado por EA5IUE. SIGHUP es el convenio habitual en daemons unix para reload de config.
close_reason: Implementado: SIGHUP recarga ft8ctrl.yaml en caliente (selectores, BlackList, retry_time, tx_retries, tx_power, follow_frequency). Config invalida se ignora conservando la anterior; campos de sockets/BD/identidad avisan y requieren reinicio. Tests con -race y README actualizado.

title: Unit & integration tests
description: Add Go tests for the critical, easy-to-regress pieces: protocol round-trip encode/decode against known WSJT-X byte sequences and Julian/grid time conversions; geo distance/azimuth/grid2latlon against the Python results; get_band mapping; DB upsert/status/delete/purge semantics; and selector filtering/sorting logic. Aim for parity with the Python behavior.
notes: Parity + integration tests added (test-only files). internal/wsjtx/golden_test.go: exact-byte golden for header/HaltTx/FreeText, Reply field-order (client id AUTOFT), hand-built Status datagram round-trip (the previously un-round-tripped packet), Julian/wstime/float64 hand-computed. internal/geo/geo_parity_test.go: distance/azimuth re-derived from geo.py formulas + maidenhead grids (CM87/FN20/AA00/sub-square). internal/db/band_parity_test.go: every get_band entry + truncation boundary 28.999->10/29.0->0. internal/integration/integration_test.go (new pkg): E2E wsjtx->db->selector (insert CO8LY/G3XYZ, Any chain picks higher SNR, mark worked -> drops from pool + WorkedCountries), and config.Load(sample)->selector.Build wiring path. No parity discrepancies found. All target packages green + go vet clean.
close_reason: Main wiring + parity/integration tests done; full module builds/vets/tests clean (incl E2E smoke of ft8ctrl).

title: ft8ctrl main command (CLI, logging, wiring)
description: Port main() from ft8ctrl.py: parse flags (-c/--config), set up logging (console level via LOG_LEVEL env + rotating debug logfile equivalent), initialize the DB, start the DB-insert and purge goroutines, build the configured selector chain, and run the Sequencer. Wire all packages into the ft8ctrl binary with graceful Ctrl-C shutdown.
notes: cmd/ft8ctrl/main.go: wiring final. Flags -c/--config; loadConfig (Load path or Find). internal/log.Setup: fanout slog.Handler -> console (level from LOG_LEVEL env, default INFO) + size-rotating file handler at DEBUG (2MiB/5 backups, replaces RotatingFileHandler, no external dep); default logfile ft8ctrl-debug.log; ~ expansion. Pipeline: db.Open -> dxcc.New -> db.NewWriter; signal.NotifyContext(SIGINT,SIGTERM); cmds chan(256); go writer.Run(ctx,cmds); go runPurge(ctx, every 60s, retry_time min); LOTW loaded once only if needsLOTW(any selector lotw_users_only); selector.Build(call_selector, Selectors, Deps{store,blacklist.New(cfg.BlackList),lotw,log}); sequencer.New(cfg.FT8Ctrl,chain,cmds,log).Run(ctx). Graceful shutdown on signal (ctx cancel propagates to writer/purge/sequencer). Smoke E2E verified: binds 127.0.0.1:22399, writer+purge threads start, purge ticks, clean SIGINT exit 0. internal/log has its own tests (parseLevel, rotation, setup).
close_reason: Main wiring + parity/integration tests done; full module builds/vets/tests clean (incl E2E smoke of ft8ctrl).

title: Call-selector plugins (Any/CallSign/Grid/Continent/Country/CQZone/ITUZone/DXCC100/Extra)
description: Port every selector plugin on top of the framework: Any; CallSign (regexp + list, reverse); Grid (regexp, reverse); Continent and Country (list, reverse, valid-entity check); CQZone/ITUZone (integer zone list, reverse); DXCC100 (skip countries already worked >= worked_count on band); Extra (POTA/DX/etc match, reverse). Register each under its config-section name and honor per-selector min_snr/max_snr/lotw_users_only/reverse options.
notes: internal/selector/plugins.go: los 9 selectores (Any, CallSign, Grid, Continent, Country, CQZone, ITUZone, DXCC100, Extra) embebiendo *Base, con init() que Register cada nombre. XOR Python (cond)^reverse -> Go (cond != reverse). CallSign/Grid compilan regexp en el constructor (error si inválido); grids vacíos se saltan. DXCC100 captura deps.Store y usa WorkedCountries(band,worked_count) (default 2). FIX dos bugs de zones.py (con comentarios): (1) z_get recursaba infinito (self.get) -> ahora itera b.Candidates(band); (2) comparaba set de strings vs zona int del DB (nunca matcheaba) -> parsea zonas a int con strconv.Atoi y compara int-vs-int; CQZone/ITUZone comparten zonePlugin parametrizado. DEVIACIÓN: Country omite la validación dxcc.isentity (el framework no expone DXCC en construcción; el filtro solo necesita membership de string). 18 tests de plugins pasan.
close_reason: 9 selector plugins implemented, registered and tested (18 tests); full module builds/vets/tests clean.

title: Call-selector framework (interface, registry, base logic)
description: Port the CallSelector ABC and shared selection logic from plugins/base.py. Go has no dynamic import, so replace LoadPlugins with a name->constructor registry and a Selector interface (Get(band) -> record). Implement the base behavior: query cqcalls (status=0, band, recent window), short-lived result cache (SingleObjectCache equiv), coefficient = distance*10^(snr/10), sort by SNR desc, and select_record filtering by min/max SNR, blacklist, and LOTW membership. Handle the DX-to-same-continent ignore rule.
notes: internal/selector framework. selector.go: Selector interface (Name/Get(band)->(Candidate,bool)); Candidate=db.Record+Coef; Selection=Candidate+selector name; Chain.Select (first non-nil wins, replaces LoadPlugins.__call__); Membership interface + everything{} (=Python Nothing); registry name->Constructor with Register()/Registered()/Build(names,cfgs,deps) replacing dynamic import; Deps{Store,Blacklist,LOTW,Log}. base.go: Base + NewBase(cfg,deps) resolving defaults (min_snr -50/max_snr 50/delta 29s/my_continent NA, lotw_users_only-> real LOTW else everything). Candidates(band): cached-per-band (TTL 3s, CacheTTL field, 0=disabled) query store.Recent(status=0/band/time>now-delta), drops extra=='DX' && continent==my_continent, attaches coefficient=dist*10^(snr/10). SelectRecord: stable sort SNR desc, return first passing min<snr<max && !blacklist && lotw. Plugins (.10) embed *Base and implement Get; register via init(). DEVIATION: replaced Python SingleObjectCache (shared one cache across all bands due to a quirk) with a correct per-band TTL cache. 8 tests pass (sort, blacklist, snr bounds, DX filter, LOTW filter, chain order, build unknown/registered).
close_reason: Selector framework (interface, registry, base query+filter+select) implemented and tested (8 tests); module clean.

title: LOTW user-activity cache & lookup
description: Port the LOTW piece of plugins/base.py: download https://lotw.arrl.org/lotw-user-activity.csv (TLS, cert verification disabled like the original), cache it on disk with a 7-day expiry, keep only users seen within LOTW_LASTSEEN (270) days, and expose a fast membership test (call in LOTW). Replace the Python dbm/marshal cache with an idiomatic Go store.
notes: internal/lotw: Cache with in-memory uppercased call set. New(path,opts)/Default(); Contains (case-insensitive); rebuilds (download+filter+persist) when cache missing/older than 7d. Keeps users seen within 270d. Download uses InsecureSkipVerify (matches Python ssl._create_unverified_context, //nosec noted). Persist via encoding/gob {Stamp,Users}. Pure parseActivity(io.Reader,cutoff) + injectable fetch/now seams for network-free tests. 5 tests pass.
close_reason: Ported and unit-tested; full module builds, vets and tests clean.

title: DXCC entity lookup (port of DXEntity)
description: Replace the Python DXEntity dependency: given a callsign/prefix, return DXCC country, continent, CQ zone, and ITU zone, and support entity listing/prefix queries (used by countries.py and the DB enrichment). Port or vendor a cty.dat-based lookup in Go (evaluate existing Go ham-radio libs vs. porting). Provide lookup, isentity, entities, and get_entity equivalents.
notes: internal/dxcc: replaces Python DXEntity. Vendored FULL big-CTY cty.dat from country-files.com/bigcty/cty.dat (346 entities) into data/cty.dat, embedded via go:embed. Parser handles header (8 colon fields, *=non-DXCC prefix), comma alias list with overrides =CALL (exact), (n) CQ, [n] ITU, {Continent}, <lat/lon>/~ref~ ignored. Lookup: uppercase -> exact =CALL -> longest-prefix match -> ErrNotFound. API: New()/Lookup/Entities/IsEntity/GetEntity, Entity{Prefix,Country,Continent,CQZone,ITUZone}. NOTE for callers: Germany name is 'Fed. Rep. of Germany'; prefix match is greedy (real DXCC behavior). Tests pass (W6BSD/G/JA/VK/CO/DL/VE).
close_reason: Ported and unit-tested; full module builds, vets and tests clean.

title: SQLite persistence layer (cqcalls table)
description: Port dbutils.py: create the cqcalls schema (call, extra, time, status, snr, grid, lat, lon, distance, azimuth, country, continent, cqzone, ituzone, frequency, band, packet JSON) with the unique (call,band) index. Implement INSERT (upsert on conflict, skip when status=2 worked), STATUS update, DELETE, and the periodic Purge of stale unworked rows. Implement get_band frequency->band mapping and the get_call query. Replace the Python thread+Queue design with a goroutine consuming a channel of DB commands. Decide on JSON encoding of the stored decode packet.
notes: internal/db (modernc.org/sqlite, pure-Go). store.go: Open (WAL+busy_timeout=15s+synchronous=NORMAL, creates cqcalls schema verbatim from dbutils.py incl unique(call,band) + time/grid indexes), Close, GetCall, Recent(band,since) [selector base query status=0/band/time>], WorkedCountries(band,minCount) [DXCC100], DeleteCallBand [lookup tool], Purge(retry) [status<2 AND time<datetime('now',-N min)]. Record = full row. writer.go: Command interface (InsertCmd/StatusCmd/DeleteCmd) replacing the Python (DBCommand,dict) Queue tuples; Writer.Run(ctx,<-chan Command) single-goroutine consumer + Writer.Process(cmd) synchronous (testable). Insert enriches via geo (GridToLatLon/Distance/Azimuth from my_grid origin) + dxcc.Lookup (skips fake calls on ErrNotFound, matches Python KeyError skip), then upsert: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(call,band) DO UPDATE SET snr,packet WHERE status<>2 (worked rows frozen). packet.go: Packet (decode spot) stored as standard JSON time.Time (NOT wire-compatible with Python's custom datetime codec — fresh DB, documented). band.go: Band(freqHz) MHz->meters map. Time column stored UTC 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' to match sqlite datetime() for purge compares. 9 tests pass (band, insert+enrich, upsert-until-worked, status terminal at 2, delete-only-status1, recent/workedcountries, purge, deletecallband, fake-call skip).
close_reason: SQLite cqcalls layer + command-channel writer + purge implemented and tested (9 tests); full module builds/vets/tests clean.

title: YAML configuration loader
description: Port config.py: load ft8ctrl.yaml (search /etc, ~/.local/etc, . or an explicit --config path). Expose the ft8ctrl section (my_call, my_grid, db_name, wsjt_ip, wsjt_port, follow_frequency, retry_time, tx_power, tx_retries, call_selector list) plus per-selector sections (Any, Grid, CallSign, CQZone, ITUZone, Country, Continent, DXCC100, Extra) and the BlackList. Use typed structs with sensible defaults rather than the Python dynamic-attribute pattern. Must stay compatible with the existing ft8ctrl.yaml.sample.
notes: internal/config: typed Config{FT8Ctrl, BlackList (uppercased), Selectors map[string]SelectorConfig}. Load(path)/Find() search /etc,~/.local/etc,.; ~ expansion. SelectorConfig has all selector keys; MinSNR/MaxSNR are *int so 'unset' is distinguishable (base defaults -50/+50 left to callers). StringList custom UnmarshalYAML coerces int|string scalars and single-scalar->1-elem list (handles zone int lists and single call_selector). Uses yaml.v3 (now in go.mod). FIX: removed duplicate 'min_snr: -18' under Extra: in repo-root ft8ctrl.yaml.sample (yaml.v3 errors on dup keys; Python kept last=1). 3 tests pass.
close_reason: Ported and unit-tested; full module builds, vets and tests clean.

title: Spherical geometry & Maidenhead grid package
description: Port geo.py: haversine great-circle distance (km), azimuth/bearing between two lat/lon points, and grid2latlon Maidenhead locator (2/4/6/8 char) to lat/lon conversion. Pure functions; add caching equivalent to the Python lru_cache if beneficial.
notes: internal/geo: Point{Lat,Lon}; Distance (haversine, km, r=6371), Azimuth (int, replicates Python abs(int(brng)) truncation), GridToLatLon (2/4/6/8 chars; ''->{0,0}; error on bad length). Stdlib only. Table tests pass.
close_reason: Ported and unit-tested; full module builds, vets and tests clean.

title: TEST probe issue
description: probe
close_reason: probe

title: cmd/adif command + docs
description: New command cmd/adif: 'bin/adif -c ft8ctrl.yaml mylog.adi [--dry-run]'. Loads config, opens the store, builds the DXCC resolver + Writer (origin from my_grid), parses the ADIF file (internal/adif), maps each record (call, band, grid, freq, date/time) and imports it as worked via the db import path. Prints a summary: total QSOs, imported, skipped (with reasons), and a per-band breakdown. --dry-run parses and reports without writing. Add to the Makefile CMDS list and the release matrix. README: a section documenting the importer and a note that it seeds DXCC100 / already-worked state. Tests: end-to-end import of a small sample ADIF into a temp DB, dry-run writes nothing.
notes: Done. cmd/adif command: 'adif -c cfg [--dry-run] <file.adi>'. Loads config, opens store, builds DXCC resolver + Writer (origin from my_grid), streams the ADIF via adif.NewReader, maps each record (call, band from BAND name or FREQ fallback, grid, freq, QSO_DATE+TIME_ON, country/cqz/cont fallback) and imports as worked via Writer.ImportWorked. Prints summary: parsed / imported / skipped(reasons) / per-band. --dry-run parses+reports without writing. Added to Makefile CMDS (+release matrix). README: command list entry + dedicated section. Verified on the real file: parsed 7908, would import 7907, 1 skipped (no band), realistic per-band split. Tests: parseFreqHz, parseQSOTime, recordToQSO (incl. no-call/no-band/freq-fallback), formatBands, end-to-end parse->import into temp store.
close_reason: cmd/adif command + docs implemented and tested; verified on the real 7908-QSO log

title: Document the TUI mode (README + help)
description: Update README with a section on 'ft8ctrl --tui': screenshot/ascii of the BlueBEEP-style layout, key bindings / function-key reference, and notes on live parameter editing, pause, search and candidate ranking. Add an in-TUI F1 help screen.
notes: Done. internal/tui/help.go: F1 help overlay (shadowed dialog, banner + key bindings table), wired into model (help state, F1 toggles, Esc/F1/q close). README: new 'Interactive TUI' section with ascii layout, panel descriptions, key-binding table, and a note that F4 edits apply live (not persisted to YAML; SIGHUP/edit file to persist). Tests: helpView dims/content, F1 open/Esc close.
close_reason: F1 help screen + README TUI docs

title: TUI: autopilot pause/resume control + state indicator
description: Wire the F2 / hotkey to Sequencer.Pause()/Resume() and reflect RUNNING/PAUSED prominently in the status panel (and color). Confirm that while paused no station is called but spots keep flowing into the DB.
notes: Done. F2 (and Space) toggles Sequencer.TogglePause() and refreshes immediately; Sequencer.Status() now overrides .Paused with the live atomic so the ●RUNNING/■PAUSED indicator flips instantly without waiting for the next publish. Nil-Seq is handled gracefully. Tests: F2 toggles a real ephemeral-port sequencer, F2 no-panic with nil Seq (plus the RUNNING/PAUSED indicator tests from tt5).
close_reason: F2 pause toggle wired + instant indicator, tested

title: TUI: ranked candidates view
description: View that lists the current band's candidates in best-to-worst order using the selector ranking API: call, country, SNR, distance, coefficient, zones, and the selector that would pick each. Live-refreshing. This is the centerpiece war-dialer 'scan' list.
notes: Done. internal/tui/candidates.go: F5 full-screen ranked candidates view — detailed scrollable table (marker/CALL/SNR/KM/CQ/ITU/GRID/STATUS/COUNTRY) over m.ranked, chosen ▶ highlighted, eligible plain, ineligible dimmed with reason (ok/blacklist/snr/lotw). ↑/↓/PgUp/PgDn scroll, Esc/F5/q close, refreshes each tick. Wired into model (candidates state + candOffset, F5 toggles). Tests: layout/dims/header/marker/reason, scroll clamp, F5 open/scroll/close. Snapshot verified.
close_reason: Full-screen ranked candidates view implemented and tested

title: TUI: database search modal + results
description: Search input dialog + results table wired to the DB search helpers: query by call/country/grid/zone/status/band and display matching cqcalls rows (with lotw column where available). BlueBEEP-styled. Allow paging/scrolling results.
notes: Done. internal/tui/search.go: searchModal (bubbles textinput + scrollable results table) opened with F3, Esc closes, Enter runs Store.Search, ↑/↓/PgUp/PgDn scroll. Rendered as a shadowed box() centred on the desktop. renderRecords table (CALL/SNR/BAND/ST/GRID/COUNTRY, worked rows tinted green, statusLabel new/wip/wkd). Wired into model (searching state routes keys to modal, Ctrl-C still quits). Added bubbles/textinput dep. Tests: run query (real store), no-store, scroll clamp, typing (focused), renderRecords dims/labels, F3 open/Esc close, statusLabel. Snapshot verified.
close_reason: Search modal + results table implemented, wired to Store.Search, tested

title: TUI: parameter editor modal
description: Modal dialog (BlueBEEP-styled) to view and edit live parameters, wired to the live-parameter control API: tx_power, tx_retries, follow_frequency, selector chain order, blacklist, retry_time. Validate input, apply via the control surface, show confirmation/errors. Immutable fields shown read-only.
notes: Done. internal/tui/params.go: paramModal (F4) — a form of bubbles textinputs for tx_power/tx_retries/follow_frequency/retry_time/selectors(chain)/blacklist, loaded from Controller.Params(), ↑/↓ navigation (wraps), Enter parses+validates+Controller.Apply (errors shown inline, e.g. unknown selector), Esc cancels. Rendered as a shadowed centred dialog. Wired into model (editing state, F4). Tests: open loads values, apply live updates controller, invalid number, unknown-selector error, navigation wrap, F4 open/Esc close, parseBool. Snapshot verified.
close_reason: Parameter editor modal implemented, wired to Controller, tested

title: BlueBEEP visual theme: palette, borders, banner, F-key bar
description: Implement the retro DOS / Turbo Vision aesthetic with lipgloss: blue background, cyan/yellow/white foreground palette, double-line box-drawing borders with drop shadows, an ANSI-art banner header ('FT8 CoPilot', war-dialer vibe), and a bottom function-key menu bar (F1 Help, F2 Pause, F3 Search, F4 Params, F5 Candidates, F10 Quit). Provide reusable styled box/panel/dialog components for the other TUI views. Reference: github.com/Jimvin/BlueBEEP.
notes: Done. New internal/tui/theme.go with the DOS/Turbo Vision primitives: palette (desktop/panel blue, cyan borders, yellow titles, status green/red), box(title,body,w,h,focused) double-line panel with the title set into the top border + exact w×h clipping/padding via fit(), shadow() drop-shadow helper for modals, functionBar(width,keys) cyan F-key bar, banner() ASCII wordmark, and shared styles (stDesktop/stHeader/stText/stDimText/stAccent). model.go refactored onto these. Tests in theme_test.go (box dims, title-too-wide fallback, fit clip/pad, function bar width). Snapshot verified visually.
close_reason: Visual theme primitives implemented and tested; model.go refactored onto them

title: Database search helpers for the TUI
description: db.Store already has GetCall, All, FindByStatus, FindByCountry, Since, Recent. Add any missing flexible-search helpers the TUI needs (e.g. search by call regexp, grid prefix, CQ/ITU zone, combined band+status filters) returning []Record. Prefer extending existing query patterns; keep it read-only and indexed where possible.
notes: Done. db.Store.Search(Query) with Query{Text (OR-match call/country/grid via LIKE %text%), Status *int, Band *int, Limit int} — all optional, ANDed, ORDER BY time DESC. Read-only, relies on SQLite case-insensitive ASCII LIKE. Tests: call substring (recent-first order), country, band+status filter, limit, empty=all.
close_reason: Flexible Store.Search helper implemented and tested

title: Documentation, packaging & config sample
description: Write README for the Go port (build, install, usage, macOS/Linux notes), ship the ft8ctrl.yaml.sample, document the selector plugins and options, and provide release/build packaging (static binaries). Carry over the AppleScript logging/CQ snippets and LICENSE/attribution to Fred W6BSD (BSD-3-Clause).

title: countries DXCC CLI tool
description: Port countries.py: a CLI helper over the DXCC lookup — list all entities (--list), resolve a callsign/prefix to country+continent+zones (--country), check an entity exists (--check), and list all prefixes for an entity (--prefix).
notes: cmd/countries: port of countries.py over internal/dxcc. flag-based -l/--list, -c/--country (resolve call/prefix -> 'Prefix: X > P = Country - Continent..., CQZone, ITUZone'), -C/--check (IsEntity, case-insensitive->canonical), -p/--prefix (GetEntity, wrapped ' >  ' indent). Exactly-one-flag enforced; errors to stderr + exit 1. Smoke-tested all flags offline (embedded cty.dat). NOTE: countries.py had -c/-p handlers cross-wired; implemented the sane mapping per spec (-c resolves call, -p lists prefixes).
close_reason: Implemented and tested; full module builds/vets/tests clean, CLIs smoke-tested.

title: lookup status CLI tool
description: Port lookup.py: a CLI to inspect the cqcalls DB — continuous refreshing table view (--run), filter by call (regexp) / country / status, optional band filter, and delete a record by call+band. Render tabular output (tabulate equivalent) and annotate each row with LOTW membership.
notes: cmd/lookup: port of lookup.py over internal/db+config+lotw. Added db query helpers to store.go: FindByStatus/FindByCountry/All (optional *int band) + Since(d) for --run. --call filters via Go regexp.MatchString (re.search semantics) over All() instead of registering a SQLite REGEXP fn. Actions (exactly one): -d/--delete (needs -b), -r/--run (refresh every 15s, window --interval default 30s, clears screen), -c/--call, --country, --status; optional -b/--band. Table via text/tabwriter (renderTable extracted + unit-tested) with lotw column. LOTW via lotw.Default(); on error logs stderr warning + continues disabled (offline-safe). BUGFIX in shared scanRecord: modernc returns TIMESTAMP-affinity column as RFC3339 so Record.Time was zero; added RFC3339 fallback parse (broadening, existing db tests still pass).
